pages.desc: A single large brushed Chinese character 美 (měi, "beauty") in bold semi-cursive calligraphy, filling the leaf, captioned in Thai รูป ๑ ความงาม ("figure 1, beauty"). This opens the Chinese-arts book of
pages.descFull: A single large brushed Chinese character 美 (měi, "beauty") in bold semi-cursive calligraphy, filling the leaf, captioned in Thai รูป ๑ ความงาม ("figure 1, beauty"). This opens the Chinese-arts book of auspicious lucky-words: the character itself is the charm, a brush-written wish for beauty to be displayed, hung or copied.

pages.desc: A single large brushed Chinese character 調 (tiáo, "harmony / attunement") in bold calligraphy, captioned in Thai รูป ๒ ความกลมกลืน ("figure 2, harmony / blending-together"). A lucky-word for concord a
pages.descFull: A single large brushed Chinese character 調 (tiáo, "harmony / attunement") in bold calligraphy, captioned in Thai รูป ๒ ความกลมกลืน ("figure 2, harmony / blending-together"). A lucky-word for concord and balanced relations, presented as brush-art to be displayed or traced.

pages.desc: Two brushed Chinese characters 桃花 (táohuā, "peach blossom") stacked vertically in bold calligraphy, captioned in Thai รูป ๓ ดอกท้อ ("figure 3, peach flower"). Peach blossom is the classic Chinese embl
pages.descFull: Two brushed Chinese characters 桃花 (táohuā, "peach blossom") stacked vertically in bold calligraphy, captioned in Thai รูป ๓ ดอกท้อ ("figure 3, peach flower"). Peach blossom is the classic Chinese emblem of romance and love-luck (the taohua "peach-blossom fortune") — a charm-word for attraction and affairs of the heart.

pages.desc: Two brushed Chinese characters 勁竹 (jìng zhú, "vigorous / strong bamboo") in bold calligraphy, with a small Thai caption glossing it as bamboo (รูป ๔, ไผ่). Bamboo is the emblem of upright resilience —
pages.descFull: Two brushed Chinese characters 勁竹 (jìng zhú, "vigorous / strong bamboo") in bold calligraphy, with a small Thai caption glossing it as bamboo (รูป ๔, ไผ่). Bamboo is the emblem of upright resilience — bending without breaking, strength through hardship — offered here as an auspicious lucky-word.

pages.desc: Two brushed Chinese characters 風柳 (fēng liǔ, "wind-willow / willow in the wind") in bold calligraphy, with a faint Thai caption (รูป ๗). The willow bending in the wind is an emblem of grace, supplenes
pages.descFull: Two brushed Chinese characters 風柳 (fēng liǔ, "wind-willow / willow in the wind") in bold calligraphy, with a faint Thai caption (รูป ๗). The willow bending in the wind is an emblem of grace, suppleness and yielding strength — a lucky-word for graceful adaptability.

pages.desc: A vertical column of brushed Chinese characters read as an auspicious wealth-phrase (小/少 腹 大 富, "small means, great riches"), captioned in Thai รูป ๘ เงินน้อย โชคลาภ ("figure 8, little money — windfal
pages.descFull: A vertical column of brushed Chinese characters read as an auspicious wealth-phrase (小/少 腹 大 富, "small means, great riches"), captioned in Thai รูป ๘ เงินน้อย โชคลาภ ("figure 8, little money — windfall fortune"). A prosperity charm-phrase promising great fortune from small beginnings, written as display calligraphy.

pages.desc: A decorative calligraphy plate. Three Chinese characters, brushed in bold black ink and stacked vertically down the centre of an otherwise blank page, read 銀杏葉 ("ginkgo leaf"). Beneath them an italic
pages.descFull: A decorative calligraphy plate. Three Chinese characters, brushed in bold black ink and stacked vertically down the centre of an otherwise blank page, read 銀杏葉 ("ginkgo leaf"). Beneath them an italic Thai caption gives the figure number and gloss: "รูป 9 ใบแปะก๊วย" (Figure 9, ginkgo leaf). No chart, grid or human figure appears — this is a single brush-written phrase illustrating an image from the surrounding beauty text rather than a fortune-telling diagram.

pages.desc: A decorative calligraphy plate. Three Chinese characters brushed in heavy black ink run vertically down the centre of a blank page, reading 高跟鞋 ("high-heeled shoe"). An italic Thai caption below gives
pages.descFull: A decorative calligraphy plate. Three Chinese characters brushed in heavy black ink run vertically down the centre of a blank page, reading 高跟鞋 ("high-heeled shoe"). An italic Thai caption below gives the figure number and gloss: "รูป 10 รองเท้าส้นสูง" (Figure 10, high-heeled shoes). The page carries no chart, grid or illustration beyond the inscription itself; it is a single ink phrase illustrating an image from the surrounding text.

pages.desc: A decorative calligraphy plate. Four Chinese characters brushed in black ink descend the centre of the page, reading 玉露珠潤 (roughly "jade dew, pearl-lustrous"). Below runs an italic Thai caption: "รูป
pages.descFull: A decorative calligraphy plate. Four Chinese characters brushed in black ink descend the centre of the page, reading 玉露珠潤 (roughly "jade dew, pearl-lustrous"). Below runs an italic Thai caption: "รูป 11 หยาดน้ำค้างยามเช้าที่ลื่นราวหยก" (Figure 11, morning dewdrops as smooth/glossy as jade). The rest of the sheet is blank. It is a single ink inscription rendering a poetic simile from the text, not a face-reading, mole or feng-shui chart.

pages.desc: A decorative calligraphy plate. Two Chinese characters brushed boldly in black ink sit centred on a blank page, reading 絲瀑 ("silk cascade / waterfall of silk"). An italic Thai caption beneath gives th
pages.descFull: A decorative calligraphy plate. Two Chinese characters brushed boldly in black ink sit centred on a blank page, reading 絲瀑 ("silk cascade / waterfall of silk"). An italic Thai caption beneath gives the figure number and gloss: "รูป 12 น้ำตกไหม" (Figure 12, silk waterfall). No diagram, grid or figure accompanies the characters; the page is a single brush-written phrase illustrating an image from the surrounding beauty text.

pages.desc: A decorative calligraphy plate. Two Chinese characters brushed in black ink run vertically down the page, reading 賽雪 (literally "rival snow," i.e. whiter than snow). An italic Thai caption below reads
pages.descFull: A decorative calligraphy plate. Two Chinese characters brushed in black ink run vertically down the page, reading 賽雪 (literally "rival snow," i.e. whiter than snow). An italic Thai caption below reads "รูป 14 ขาวกว่าหิมะ" (Figure 14, whiter than snow). The printed page number 54 and a vertical Thai running-head appear in the left margin. The sheet holds only this inscription — no chart or illustration — a single brush phrase illustrating the text.

pages.desc: A decorative calligraphy plate. Two Chinese characters brushed in bold black ink are centred on the page, reading 似綢 ("like silk / satin"). An italic Thai caption below gives the figure and gloss: "รู
pages.descFull: A decorative calligraphy plate. Two Chinese characters brushed in bold black ink are centred on the page, reading 似綢 ("like silk / satin"). An italic Thai caption below gives the figure and gloss: "รูป 16 ลื่นราวไหม" (Figure 16, smooth as silk). The right margin shows the page number 59 and the English running-head "Beauty Feng Shui." No chart or diagram appears; the page is a single brush-written phrase illustrating the surrounding text.

pages.desc: A decorative calligraphy plate. Two Chinese characters brushed in heavy black ink run vertically down the page, reading 恢復 ("recovery / restoration"). Below, an italic Thai caption reads "รูป 17 การฟื
pages.descFull: A decorative calligraphy plate. Two Chinese characters brushed in heavy black ink run vertically down the page, reading 恢復 ("recovery / restoration"). Below, an italic Thai caption reads "รูป 17 การฟื้นตัว" (Figure 17, recovery / recuperation). The printed page number 62 and a vertical Thai running-head sit in the left margin. The sheet carries only this inscription — no chart, grid or illustration — a single brush phrase used to illustrate the text.

pages.desc: A decorative calligraphy plate. A single Chinese character brushed in bold black ink, formed as a closed box-like frame, is centred on the page, reading 固 ("firm / solid / stable"). An italic Thai cap
pages.descFull: A decorative calligraphy plate. A single Chinese character brushed in bold black ink, formed as a closed box-like frame, is centred on the page, reading 固 ("firm / solid / stable"). An italic Thai caption below reads "รูป 18 เสถียรภาพ" (Figure 18, stability). The right margin carries the page number 63 and the English running-head "Beauty Feng Shui." No diagram or chart accompanies it; the page is a single ink inscription illustrating the text's theme of stability.

pages.desc: A full-page calligraphy plate: two large Chinese characters brush-written in black ink and stacked vertically, reading 調合 (tiáo hé, 'to blend / harmonize / mix in proportion'). The upper character 調 c
pages.descFull: A full-page calligraphy plate: two large Chinese characters brush-written in black ink and stacked vertically, reading 調合 (tiáo hé, 'to blend / harmonize / mix in proportion'). The upper character 調 carries the 言 speech radical at its left; the lower character is 合. Beneath, the Thai caption reads 'รูป 19 การกำกับ' (roughly 'Figure 19, regulating / directing'). A grey side-tab at the outer edge prints the running head 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 65. One of a numbered series of single-word calligraphy figures in this chapter.

pages.desc: A full-page calligraphy plate: two large brush-drawn Chinese characters in black ink, stacked vertically, reading 明亮 (míng liàng, 'bright / luminous / clear'). The top character 明 is built from 日 (sun
pages.descFull: A full-page calligraphy plate: two large brush-drawn Chinese characters in black ink, stacked vertically, reading 明亮 (míng liàng, 'bright / luminous / clear'). The top character 明 is built from 日 (sun) beside 月 (moon); the lower character is 亮. Below sits the Thai caption 'รูป 21 สว่างกระจ่างใส' ('Figure 21, bright and radiantly clear'). A grey side-tab carries the running head 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 69. Part of the numbered run of calligraphy figures.

pages.desc: A full-page calligraphy plate: two large brush-written Chinese characters in black ink, stacked vertically, reading 循環 (xún huán, 'circulation / cycle / to rotate through'). The upper character 循 has
pages.descFull: A full-page calligraphy plate: two large brush-written Chinese characters in black ink, stacked vertically, reading 循環 (xún huán, 'circulation / cycle / to rotate through'). The upper character 循 has the 彳 'step' radical at its left; the lower 環 carries the 王/玉 (jade) radical. The Thai caption reads 'รูป 22 การผลัดเปลี่ยนหมุนเวียน' ('Figure 22, cyclical rotation / alternation'). A grey side-tab shows Thai running-head text and the page number 70.

pages.desc: A full-page calligraphy plate: two large brush-drawn Chinese characters in black ink, stacked vertically. The upper character closely resembles 南 (nán); the lower is a broad, cross-stroked character.
pages.descFull: A full-page calligraphy plate: two large brush-drawn Chinese characters in black ink, stacked vertically. The upper character closely resembles 南 (nán); the lower is a broad, cross-stroked character. Beneath, the Thai caption reads 'รูป 25 การให้' ('Figure 25, giving / generosity'). This plate opens a short run of Buddhist-themed calligraphy figures (the following pages carry mantra text), so the wording most plausibly renders a term for almsgiving (dāna). A grey side-tab prints Thai running-head text and the page number 78.

pages.desc: A full-page calligraphy plate: nine large brush-drawn Chinese characters set in three vertical columns, read right-to-left and top-to-bottom as 南無摩訶般若波羅蜜 (Nánwú móhē bōrě bōluómì) — the invocation 'Na
pages.descFull: A full-page calligraphy plate: nine large brush-drawn Chinese characters set in three vertical columns, read right-to-left and top-to-bottom as 南無摩訶般若波羅蜜 (Nánwú móhē bōrě bōluómì) — the invocation 'Namo Mahāprajñāpāramitā,' homage to the Great Perfection of Wisdom. The Thai caption reads 'รูป 26 ภูมิปัญญาอันยิ่งใหญ่บรรลุถึงอีกฝั่งหนึ่ง' ('Figure 26, the great wisdom that reaches the far shore'). A grey side-tab shows the running head 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 79.

pages.desc: A full-page calligraphy plate: brush-drawn Chinese characters in black ink arranged in two vertical columns, forming a transliterated mantra. The right column reads downward from 唵 (oṃ) through 烏倫尼 (u
pages.descFull: A full-page calligraphy plate: brush-drawn Chinese characters in black ink arranged in two vertical columns, forming a transliterated mantra. The right column reads downward from 唵 (oṃ) through 烏倫尼 (u-lun-ni…); the left column ends in a 婆訶 (svāhā / -pó-hē) closing formula. Beneath, the Thai caption reads 'รูป 27 หัวใจที่ไม่สามารถแหลกสลาย' ('Figure 27, the heart/essence that cannot be shattered'). A grey side-tab carries Thai running-head text and the page number 80.

pages.desc: A full-page calligraphy plate: a longer transliterated mantra of brush-drawn Chinese characters in black ink, set in vertical columns and read right-to-left. It renders a dhāraṇī built on repeated syl
pages.descFull: A full-page calligraphy plate: a longer transliterated mantra of brush-drawn Chinese characters in black ink, set in vertical columns and read right-to-left. It renders a dhāraṇī built on repeated syllables — the character 修 (a 'su/syu' sound) recurs several times amid 唵 (oṃ), 哆唎, 摩 and 薩, closing at lower left with a 婆訶 (svāhā) ending. The Thai caption reads 'รูป 28 ร่างกายปราศจากความเจ็บปวด ปราศจากความผิดพลาด ปราศจากความต้องการ ปราศจากความอยากได้' ('Figure 28, a body free of pain, free of fault, free of want, free of craving'). Side-tab: 'Beauty Feng Shui', page 81.

pages.desc: A full-page calligraphy plate dominated by a single very large brush-drawn Chinese character in black ink: 佛 (fó, 'Buddha') — the 亻 person radical at its left beside the 弗 element, one stroke drawn ou
pages.descFull: A full-page calligraphy plate dominated by a single very large brush-drawn Chinese character in black ink: 佛 (fó, 'Buddha') — the 亻 person radical at its left beside the 弗 element, one stroke drawn out into a long vertical tail sweeping down the page. Beneath, the Thai caption reads 'รูป 31 พุทธะ' ('Figure 31, the Buddha'). A grey side-tab prints the running head 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 87.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: three large Chinese characters written vertically in bold black ink — 想像力 (xiǎngxiànglì, 'the power of imagination'). Beneath them runs the Thai caption 'รูป 32 พล
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: three large Chinese characters written vertically in bold black ink — 想像力 (xiǎngxiànglì, 'the power of imagination'). Beneath them runs the Thai caption 'รูป 32 พลังแห่งจินตนาการ' ('Figure 32, the power of imagination'). The printed page number 88 and a faint vertical Thai running header sit in the left margin. This is a decorative section-divider illustration rather than an instructional chart.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate showing two large Chinese characters stacked vertically in heavy black ink — 度量 (dùliàng, roughly 'measure / tolerance / magnanimity'). Below is the Thai caption 'ร
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate showing two large Chinese characters stacked vertically in heavy black ink — 度量 (dùliàng, roughly 'measure / tolerance / magnanimity'). Below is the Thai caption 'รูป 33 จำนวนที่ถูกต้อง' ('Figure 33, the correct amount'). The page number 90 and a small vertical Thai running header appear at the left margin. A decorative divider page, not a diagram.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate with five Chinese characters set in two vertical columns of expressive black ink — 習慣成自然 (xíguàn chéng zìrán, 'habit becomes second nature'), the right column readi
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate with five Chinese characters set in two vertical columns of expressive black ink — 習慣成自然 (xíguàn chéng zìrán, 'habit becomes second nature'), the right column reading 習慣成 and the left 自然. The Thai caption below reads 'รูป 35 จากนิสัยกลายเป็นธรรมชาติที่สอง' ('Figure 35, from a habit it becomes a second nature'). The right margin carries the italic running head 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 97.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: three large Chinese characters written vertically in black ink — 內在美 (nèizàiměi, 'inner beauty'). The Thai caption beneath reads 'รูป 36 ความงามภายใน' ('Figure 36,
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: three large Chinese characters written vertically in black ink — 內在美 (nèizàiměi, 'inner beauty'). The Thai caption beneath reads 'รูป 36 ความงามภายใน' ('Figure 36, inner beauty'). A faint vertical Thai running header and the page number 98 sit in the left margin. A decorative section-divider page rather than an instructional illustration.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate showing two large Chinese characters in bold black ink — 陰陽 (yīnyáng, 'yin and yang'). The Thai caption below reads 'รูป 37 หยินและหยาง' ('Figure 37, Yin and Yang')
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate showing two large Chinese characters in bold black ink — 陰陽 (yīnyáng, 'yin and yang'). The Thai caption below reads 'รูป 37 หยินและหยาง' ('Figure 37, Yin and Yang'). The page number 100 and a small vertical Thai running header appear in the left margin. A decorative divider page introducing the yin–yang theme, not a diagram.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate with a single large Chinese character rendered in a sweeping, dry-brush stroke of black ink — 淨 (jìng, 'pure / clear / clean'). The Thai caption beneath reads 'รูป
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate with a single large Chinese character rendered in a sweeping, dry-brush stroke of black ink — 淨 (jìng, 'pure / clear / clean'). The Thai caption beneath reads 'รูป 39 ความแจ่มชัด' ('Figure 39, clarity'). The right margin bears the italic running head 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 105. A decorative section-divider page.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: four Chinese characters written vertically in fluid black ink — 養生之道 (yǎngshēng zhī dào, 'the way of nurturing life / cultivating health'). The Thai caption below
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: four Chinese characters written vertically in fluid black ink — 養生之道 (yǎngshēng zhī dào, 'the way of nurturing life / cultivating health'). The Thai caption below reads 'รูป 40 วิถีสู่สุขภาพ' ('Figure 40, the path to health'). A faint vertical Thai running header and the page number 112 appear in the left margin. A decorative divider page rather than an instructional chart.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate with a single large Chinese character in a broad, boxy black brushstroke — 圓 (yuán, 'round / complete / unity'). The Thai caption beneath reads 'รูป 41 เอกภาพ' ('Fi
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate with a single large Chinese character in a broad, boxy black brushstroke — 圓 (yuán, 'round / complete / unity'). The Thai caption beneath reads 'รูป 41 เอกภาพ' ('Figure 41, unity'). The right margin carries the italic running head 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 113. A decorative section-divider page, not a diagram.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: several Chinese characters written in bold semi-cursive strokes and arranged in vertical columns across the page. The legible characters include 氣 (qì, 'chi / vita
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: several Chinese characters written in bold semi-cursive strokes and arranged in vertical columns across the page. The legible characters include 氣 (qì, 'chi / vital energy') together with 至, 剛, 所, 不 and 在, rendering a phrase in praise of qi. The italic Thai caption reads 'รูป 43 ชี่เข้มแข็งและยิ่งใหญ่ที่สุด มันอยู่ทุกหนแห่ง' (Figure 43: chi is the strongest and greatest; it is everywhere). The English running header 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 119 sit at the right margin.

pages.desc: A single large Chinese character, 力 (lì, 'strength / power'), brushed in heavy black ink and centered on an otherwise blank page; the long diagonal downstroke breaks into dry, streaked 'flying-white'
pages.descFull: A single large Chinese character, 力 (lì, 'strength / power'), brushed in heavy black ink and centered on an otherwise blank page; the long diagonal downstroke breaks into dry, streaked 'flying-white' bristle marks. Beneath it the Thai caption reads 'รูป 46. อำนาจ' (Figure 46: power / authority). The left margin carries the page number 130 and a small vertical Thai running-head 'ฮวงจุ้ยกับความงาม' (feng shui and beauty).

pages.desc: A single large Chinese character, 健 (jiàn, 'healthy / robust'), rendered in bold, confident brushstrokes and centered on a blank page. The italic Thai caption below reads 'รูป 47 สุขภาพ' (Figure 47: h
pages.descFull: A single large Chinese character, 健 (jiàn, 'healthy / robust'), rendered in bold, confident brushstrokes and centered on a blank page. The italic Thai caption below reads 'รูป 47 สุขภาพ' (Figure 47: health). The right margin bears the English running header 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 137. The character is the page's sole image.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: a vertically stacked group of Chinese characters in fluid semi-cursive script, the uppermost legible as 輕 (qīng, 'light'); together the group corresponds to the se
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: a vertically stacked group of Chinese characters in fluid semi-cursive script, the uppermost legible as 輕 (qīng, 'light'); together the group corresponds to the sense of ease or relaxation (輕鬆). The italic Thai caption reads 'รูป 49 การผ่อนคลาย' (Figure 49: relaxation). The left margin shows the page number 140 and a small vertical Thai running-head 'ฮวงจุ้ยกับความงาม' (feng shui and beauty).

pages.desc: A single large Chinese character, 韻 (yùn, 'charm / rhyme / tone'), brushed in bold black ink and centered on a blank page. The italic Thai caption reads 'รูป 52 เสน่ห์' (Figure 52: charm / allure). Th
pages.descFull: A single large Chinese character, 韻 (yùn, 'charm / rhyme / tone'), brushed in bold black ink and centered on a blank page. The italic Thai caption reads 'รูป 52 เสน่ห์' (Figure 52: charm / allure). The left margin carries the page number 148 and a small vertical Thai running-head; the character stands alone as the page's only image.

pages.desc: A single large Chinese character, 活 (huó, 'active / lively / life'), rendered in vigorous brushstrokes with dry, streaked 'flying-white' marks and centered on a blank page. The italic Thai caption bel
pages.descFull: A single large Chinese character, 活 (huó, 'active / lively / life'), rendered in vigorous brushstrokes with dry, streaked 'flying-white' marks and centered on a blank page. The italic Thai caption below reads 'รูป 53 กิจกรรม' (Figure 53: activity). The left margin shows the page number 150 and a small vertical Thai running-head 'ฮวงจุ้ยกับความงาม' (feng shui and beauty).

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: two Chinese characters stacked vertically, 青春 (qīngchūn, 'youth / the springtime of life'), written in bold semi-cursive strokes. The italic Thai caption reads 'รู
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: two Chinese characters stacked vertically, 青春 (qīngchūn, 'youth / the springtime of life'), written in bold semi-cursive strokes. The italic Thai caption reads 'รูป 55 ความเยาว์วัย' (Figure 55: youthfulness). The right margin bears the English running header 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 153.

pages.desc: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: two Chinese characters stacked vertically, 氣質 (qìzhì, 'temperament / disposition'), brushed in bold black ink with 氣 above and 質 below. The italic Thai caption rea
pages.descFull: A full-page brush-calligraphy plate: two Chinese characters stacked vertically, 氣質 (qìzhì, 'temperament / disposition'), brushed in bold black ink with 氣 above and 質 below. The italic Thai caption reads 'รูป 57 บุคลิกภาพ' (Figure 57: personality / character). The right margin shows the English running header 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 155.

pages.desc: A single large Chinese character, 昭 (zhāo, 'bright / luminous / manifest'), rendered in bold brushstrokes and centered on a blank page. The italic Thai caption below reads 'รูป 58 เสน่ห์' (Figure 58:
pages.descFull: A single large Chinese character, 昭 (zhāo, 'bright / luminous / manifest'), rendered in bold brushstrokes and centered on a blank page. The italic Thai caption below reads 'รูป 58 เสน่ห์' (Figure 58: charm / allure). The right margin carries the English running header 'Beauty Feng Shui' and the page number 159.

items.title: Biography Of Lp Thob And His Katha
